title: [Feature Request]: No dock icon
author: sciencefictionspacecraft
body:
### Search for Similar Requests

- [x] I have searched existing issues for similar requests

### Description

Hi. I love Ice, so thanks for the app, at this point I couldn't live without it. One thing though, is that it puts a big ol icon in the Dock, which is fairly useless and undoes some of the minimalism that the app otherwise brings.

Can we have a setting to hide the dock icon? Ice is great just running quietly an unobtrusively in the menu bar.

Thanks!

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Split menu bar does not function like expected in full screen
author: jurgenvisser
body:
### Search for Similar Reports

- [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports

### Description

the wallpaper still shows in the menu bar and it isn't black on notched screens. it behaves different when you user vinger gestures on the fullscreen apps or when you go into Mission Control and then go back fullscreen.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Turn on split menu bar shape in menu bar appearance.
2. Go full screen in an app and the wallpaper still shows in the menu bar and it isn't black on notched screens.

### App Version

0.11.12

### macOS Version

Tahoe 26.4

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Starts but nothing much more
author: tsjl
body:
### Search for Similar Reports

- [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports

### Description

I can start the ICE app. At first it brong up some popup that had two buttons which did nothing.

Now when starting it hides things from the menubar and puts an ellipsis there to open them back but it seems that it doesn't do anything else. It might be that I just don't understand how to open configuration or something. And I'm sorry if that is the case. But still in that case it is quite a confusing situation.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Start the program in this computer.
2. Happends again after uninstalling and installing again.

### App Version

3.8.2 installed from Brew.

### macOS Version

26.5.1

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Scale error and auto-deploying
author: dnissanbiz
body:
### Search for Similar Reports

- [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports

### Description

On my secondary display, a lot of the time the ice bar will just appear on a very small scale that I can't even tell what the icons are. And sometimes when I click into the ice bar on either of my displays, it is absolutely massive, about seven times the size of what it usually is. this only happened in Tahoe

### Steps to Reproduce

could not figure out how to reproduce

### App Version

0.11.13-dev.2

### macOS Version

26.4.1 

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Ice in menu bar not working anymore (MacOS Golden Gate 27)
author: Bramgus12
body: ### Search for Similar Reports - [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports ### Description Just downloaded the new version of MacOS Golden Gate 27. It seems that Apple has redone some of their menu bar. One addition that I have seen now is that I can see my live activities in the menu bar (never had access to iPhone mirroring in the EU tho), which I did not see before and the battery icon seems to be redone. I have no clue if this is even fixable anymore after the update... ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Update to MacOS Golden Gate 27 2. See all icons instead of only the visible icons. ### App Version 0.11.13-dev.2 ### macOS Version 27 beta 1 ### Additional Information _No response_

title: [Bug]: Can't view the icons on Menu Bar Layout
author: fernandoJoseC
body: ### Search for Similar Reports - [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports ### Description ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Add other user from Mac 2. Open Ice app 3. Go to Menu Bar Layout ### App Version 0.11.12 ### macOS Version Tahoe 26.3 ### Additional Information _No response_

title: [Bug]: MacOS Tahoe 26.5 update broke hidden elements in ice layout
author: Elibabah
body: ### Search for Similar Reports - [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports ### Description After the latest update of MacOS Tahoe 26.5, the hidden area is not support nested elements. Instead, the icons fall into the always-hidden area, which is not needed ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Click on the elipsis icon in the bar menu to open the menu with hidden elements 2. The hidden elements do not appear 3. Open the ice settings 4. click on the layout 5. see all the hidden elements have fallen into the always hidden areas 6. Try to fix them by dragging and dropping 7. The elements fall again into the always hidden area ### App Version Version 0.11.13-dev.2 ### macOS Version MacOS Tahoe 26.5 ### Additional Information

title: [Bug]: Ligthing error when enabeling split shape in menu bar appearance
author: jurgenvisser
body: ### Search for Similar Reports - [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports ### Description The menu bar gets a brightness error. The menu bar looks to bright. this makes the feature unusable as it does not look correct. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to Menu Bar Appearance 2. Set Menu Bar > Shape Kind to Split of Full 3. Set Tint to Solid color Black (Optional) 4. The menu bar gets a brightness error ### App Version 0.11.12 ### macOS Version Tahoe 26.4 ### Additional Information _No response_

title: [Feature Request]: Option that lets you choose location of new items
author: markjaquith
body:
### Search for Similar Requests

- [x] I have searched existing issues for similar requests

### Description

I would like to choose a specific location in my Menu Bar Layout for new/unknown items to appear.

A good way to do this on the Menu Bar Layout screen is with a "new items" proxy item that can be dragged to a specific location in the Visible, Hidden, or Always-Hidden bars.

This would help with immediate visibility of just-installed things, and it would also help for tools like SwiftBar where Ice cannot seem to reliably keep track of the items when they're edited or are only sometimes displayed. By having a "new items" landing location, these unstable items could at least always end up in the same place when Ice loses track of their stable identity.

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Removed icon from tray and can't get it back
author: marcinhlybin
body:
### Search for Similar Reports

- [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports

### Description

Hi, I have removed an icon from tray while using ice by holding CMD key. 
Now I can't get it back in any way – even after quitting ice, reinstalling the software the icon belons to etc.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open ice
2. Hold CMD key and move icon outside tray zone
3. Tooltip Remove shows up
4. Icon disappears 

Might be related to this:
https://github.com/jordanbaird/Ice/issues/860#event-22288083720

### App Version

0.11.12

### macOS Version

26.3.1

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Menu bar scaling and layout issues on external/built-in displays (macOS 26.4.1)
author: etrock99
body: ### Search for Similar Reports - [x] I have searched existing issues for similar reports ### Description I am experiencing two distinct UI issues regarding the menu bar when using my MacBook Air with an external 1080p monitor. These issues seem related to display switching and resolution handling. Environment: • Device: MacBook Air M2 • macOS Version: 26.4.1 • Ice Version: 0.11.13-dev.2 • Setup: Built-in display + External 1080p monitor ### Steps to Reproduce Issue 1: Oversized Menu Bar on MacBook Air Screen Behavior: The menu bar on the MacBook Air's built-in display occasionally becomes excessively large (oversized). Steps to Reproduce/Trigger: 1. Switch focus/active windows between the built-in screen and the external screen. 2. The menu bar on the MacBook Air screen grows significantly in size. 3. If I interact with a window (e.g., resizing it), a recording prompt from Ice appears. 4. After the prompt appears, the menu bar returns to its normal size. Issue 2: Persistent Blank Space and Shrunken Bar on External 1080p Screen Behavior A (Persistent Gap): On the external 1080p monitor, there is a long, empty blank space on the left side of the menu bar. This space does not disappear and stays there indefinitely. Behavior B (Scaling Glitch): When moving the mouse cursor from the MacBook screen to the 1080p monitor, the menu bar on the 1080p screen often becomes extremely small (shrunken). ### App Version 0.11.13-dev.2 ### macOS Version 26.4.1 ### Additional Information Recovery: Similar to the first issue, it eventually restores to normal size after a brief moment or interaction, but it is easily reproducible when switching focus between screens. Expected Behavior: • The menu bar should maintain consistent and correct scaling across both displays. • There should be no persistent blank gaps on the left side of the menu bar on external monitors. • Switching focus between displays should not trigger sudden size changes (too large or too small) of the menu bar.
